problem_statement: SCCML can provide deterministic execution-chain trace semantics, but the control plane needs a witness-only boundary before accepting any SCCML kernel trace as governance proof.
success_criteria: SccmlTraceAdapterWitness validates against a public JSON schema and Foundation Mode example

SccmlTraceAdapterWitness binds instruction-trace refs, pre-state hash refs, post-state hash refs, proof refs, unsupported-operation gap refs, UAO refs, LifeMeaningJudgment refs, KernelProof refs, and TraceEntry refs

SccmlTraceAdapterWitness blocks live kernel execution, subprocess execution, external repository reads, instruction replay, state mutation, proof commitment, governance proof acceptance, unsupported-operation silence, connector calls, external writes, file writes, raw trace storage, raw state storage, raw secret storage, terminal closure, and success claims

SccmlTraceAdapterWitness rejects authority drift, unsupported-operation silence, raw trace or state retention, digest and scope drift, missing evidence refs, receipt ref drift, and summary count drift
SccmlTraceAdapterWitness is covered by a focused validator, root test, protocol manifest entry, proof coverage matrix entry, and CI hook
constraints: instruction trace, state, proof, unsupported-operation gap, and adapter manifest evidence remain digest refs
unsupported operations must be declared and cannot be ignored
KernelProof, TraceEntry, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs remain explicit
live execution, replay, mutation, and proof acceptance remain false
